title: hymenophyllum verecundum
intro: Hymenophyllum verecundum, also known as the shrinking club moss or shyness fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hymenophyllum verecundum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
